episodes.summary: Marcus spent the week learning to trust his instincts over his impulse to over-explain. When a colleague pushed back on his initial approach, he realized that cutting unnecessary words - not adding them - was what made the writing sing.
episodes.key_moment: When a colleague pushed back on his initial approach, he realized that cutting unnecessary words - not adding them - was what made the writing sing.

episodes.summary: Marcus spent the week wrestling with perfectionism, drafting copy that finally felt honest. When Devon casually announced the site was ready, Marcus felt a pang of envy at their ease - but kept quiet and approved it anyway.
episodes.key_moment: When Devon casually announced the site was ready, Marcus felt a pang of envy at their ease - but kept quiet and approved it anyway.

episodes.summary: Marcus spent the week convincing his team that imperfection tells better stories than perfection. When they finally agreed to center the copy on failed lamination layers instead of explaining them, he knew they'd cracked something real.
episodes.key_moment: When they finally agreed to center the copy on failed lamination layers instead of explaining them, he knew they'd cracked something real.
